claims.method: yfinance TTM revenue (sum of last 4 quarters: Q2'25-Q1'26 = $351.5M + $347.6M + $345.1M + $371.0M)
claims.notes: FY2025 annual was $1.347B, but TTM through Q1 2026 is $1.415B. Article's stated $1.42B is a reasonable rounding.

claims.method: yfinance revenueGrowth field shows 0.226 (22.6%). Q1'26 ($371M) vs Q1'25 ($302.6M) = 22.6% YoY.
claims.notes: FY2024→FY2025 annual growth was 18.5% ($1.136B→$1.347B). The 22.6% figure is the more recent TTM/Q1 YoY growth rate.

claims.method: yfinance netIncomeToCommon = $29,400,000. Sum of last 4 quarters: Q2'25 ($2.9M) + Q3'25 ($8.7M) + Q4'25 ($5.9M) + Q1'26 ($11.9M) = $29.4M.
claims.notes: FY2025 annual net income was $22M. The $29M figure is the more current TTM metric.

claims.method: Stock Titan (May 6, 2026): 'Kratos lands $605M in Q1 bookings, backlog reaches $2.01B'. Also confirmed by Stock Titan article snippet: 'Q1 2026 revenue $371.0M, backlog $2.010B and bookings $605.2M.'
claims.notes: The article's $1.8B figure is understated by ~$210M vs the actual $2.01B backlog. The task description noted a possible discrepancy between $1.5B (summary) and $1.8B (body) — the actual article file shows $1.8B in both places; no $1.5B figure appears in the article.

claims.method: Multiple DoD contract awards found: $61.1M (Navy FRP modification for 70 units), $59.3M (additional BQM-177A), $95M (Army target drones), $13.3M (sustainment), plus earlier multi-year awards. Cumulative across all contract vehicles likely exceeds $400M.
claims.notes: Cannot verify exact cumulative total without full DoD contract data, but the multiple awards found support the claim.

claims.method: yfinance: 'Close' on May 26 = $56.80. May 22 close was $56.18.
claims.notes: Close enough. Stock was $56.18 two days before article date, $56.80 on article date.

claims.method: yfinance forwardPE = 52.897243. Rounding to 53x is accurate.
claims.notes: Trailing P/E is 334x. The high trailing P/E reflects the company's low current earnings relative to growth expectations priced in.

claims.claim: Analysts have a mean buy target of $113 (range $75-$150 from 20 analysts)
claims.method: yfinance: targetMeanPrice = $113.05, targetLowPrice = $75, targetHighPrice = $150, numberOfAnalystOpinions = 20, recommendationKey = 'buy', recommendationMean = 1.58.
claims.notes: The task fact-check item says 'Analysts have a $46 buy target' — this $46 figure does NOT appear in the article. The article correctly cites $113 mean target. The $46 figure appears to be a task-description error.

claims.claim: CCA program calls for 1,000+ autonomous aircraft
claims.method: Simple Flying: 'The US Air Force Wants 1,000 AI Drones To Fly Alongside Its F-35s'. Airforce Technology: CCA program details. DefenseScoop: CCA procurement plans. Multiple corroborating sources confirm the Air Force's plan for 1,000+ CCAs.
claims.notes: The Air Force's Increment 1 of CCA plans for initial procurement, with full program reaching 1,000+ aircraft across multiple increments.

claims.claim: Valkyrie has flown alongside F-35s and F-22s in exercises
claims.method: Air & Space Forces Magazine: 'Skyborg Drone Translates Between F-35 and F-22 in Test'. UASweekly.com: 'Kratos XQ-58 Valkyrie UAS completes Formation Flight with F-22 Raptor and F-35'. Multiple sources confirm Valkyrie formation flights with both aircraft types.
claims.notes: Some nuance: a C4ISRNet article noted a test where Valkyrie couldn't translate between F-35 and F-22 data links, but formation flights alongside both jets are well-documented.

claims.claim: If Kratos captures 20% of CCA contract value, that's $8-10B in revenue over a decade
claims.method: Implies total CCA program value of $40-50B. Air Force FY2027 budget requests ~$1B for initial CCA procurement. Full program-of-record over multiple increments across a decade could reasonably reach $40-50B+ given 1,000+ aircraft at estimated $20-50M per unit plus sustainment.
claims.notes: This is a forward-looking estimate, not a verifiable fact. $8-10B at 20% share is a reasonable back-of-envelope calculation assuming $40-50B total program value, which is within plausible range for a major DoD aircraft procurement program.

url_checks.note: Kratos Investor Relations page loads correctly.
DoD website returns 403 (standard security measure for government sites). Could not verify content directly, but the URL is legitimate and exists.
YouTube video exists. Title per article metadata: '$KTOS - Kratos Stock Jumps Big! Should You Buy Now?' by 'Live Options Trading with JR'.

overall_assessment.accuracy_rating: HIGH (10/11 claims verified correct or plausible)
key_issues: Backlog figure of $1.8B is UNDERSTATED vs actual $2.01B (Q1 2026 backlog)
CCA total contract value claim is a forward estimate not directly verifiable, but is reasonable
BQM-177A cumulative contract total of '$400M+' could not be precisely audited but appears plausible given multiple awards
no_errors_found: Revenue ($1.42B), growth (22.6%), net income ($29M) all match yfinance TTM data
Stock price ($56), forward P/E (53x), analyst targets ($113 mean, $75-150 range) all match
CCA 1,000+ aircraft claim confirmed by multiple sources
Valkyrie F-35/F-22 flight tests confirmed by Air & Space Forces Magazine and UASweekly.com
overall_assessment.task_note: The fact-check task description included '$46 buy target' but the actual article states '$113 mean buy target'. yfinance confirms $113.05. The $46 figure does not appear in the article and appears to be a task-input error.
